summary: DHH recaps the sold-out 'Building of Basecamp' workshop, an eight-hour event that covered 37signals' development philosophy and Ruby on Rails. The audience was evenly split between developers and designers, and the broad approach spanning design techniques, MVC architecture, and business topics was well received. DHH notes the event was successful enough that they're interested in repeating it in other locations, soliciting leads for venues that could host 50 people.
key_points: The Building of Basecamp workshop sold out and was considered a great success with strong audience engagement
Core 37signals philosophies like 'Less Software' and 'Say No By Default' were central themes alongside Ruby on Rails promotion
The 50/50 developer-designer audience split validated their broad approach covering both technical and business topics
37signals saw enough demand to consider taking the workshop on the road beyond Chicago
money_quotes: The eight hours of the sold-out event flew by with tons of great questions from the audience and a real sense of shared understanding.
Besides pushing all of the underlining themes for our development process, including Less Software and Say No By Default, I got plenty of opportunity to push Ruby on Rails.

The split was about 50/50 between developers and designers, so our broad approach spanning individual design techniques, to the wonders of MVC as a structure for web-applications, to promotion and pricing of a web-based application went over very well.
key_insight: The early success of the Building of Basecamp workshop demonstrated strong demand for 37signals' opinionated development philosophy and validated their approach of bridging design, development, and business thinking.
